metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2200
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.3: "Thus, the immunoEM confirms the immunofluorescence data and demonstrates that in vivo CPG2 is concentrated in the postsynaptic site of excitatory synapses and is localized to a distinct synaptic subdomain, lateral to and beneath the PSD."

Fig.4: "Thus, the immunoEM staining pattern is consistent with our immunofluorescence finding that CPG2 is colocalized with clathrin in the postsynaptic endocytic zone and suggests that it may be involved in the endocytosis of glutamate receptors.". Experimental description: "Embryonic medium-density hippocampal cultures were made as described (Brewer et al., 1993)". This reference paper uses rats, and the cortical cultures were also made from rat;

"For postnatal high-density cortical cultures, cortices from P1 rats were plated onto poly-D-lysine and laminin-coated 6 cm dishes at 1.5 × 106 cells per dish in Neurobasal A medium (Invitrogen), supplemented with 0.5 mM L-glutamine and B27."

"Mouse monoclonal anti-CPG2 antibody 200A6.1 (IgG1) was generated against the peptide RKLESTLTGLEQSRERQERR (ResGen)"
Which refers to sigma Anti-SYNE1 Antibody, clone 200A6, MABN857.
Which in turn links to uniprot ID Q63128 (supposed brain-specific splice variant of SYNE1)

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
